strategy: Plan figures before prose drafting and bind each display item to claims, source data, and a fact sheet.

design_rules: prefer direct labels when the number of series is small
show replicate-level data when possible instead of summary-only bars
preserve editable text in vector outputs
keep source-data exports aligned with each panel

panels.purpose: Show the treatment-associated divergence in response kinetics over time.
Show the endpoint difference while preserving replicate-level observations.

design_rules: de-emphasize non-significant genes so threshold-crossing structure remains legible
use threshold guides for both fold change and adjusted significance without overpowering the data
label only prespecified sentinel genes on the volcano plot
preserve sign around zero for pathway-enrichment summaries
keep vector outputs editable and source-data exports panel-specific

panels.purpose: Show the directional differential-expression landscape and identify sentinel genes that exceed both thresholds.
Summarize the pathway-level interpretation with signed enrichment values and retained false-discovery annotations.

design_rules: keep the zero-centered fold-change reference visually obvious
de-emphasize background genes relative to labeled sentinel points
use the MA plot when abundance context matters more than significance ranking

panels.purpose: Show fold-change structure as a function of mean abundance.

design_rules: encode condition and batch with separate channels
reserve direct sample labels for a small number of noteworthy points
keep axes and origin references visible for orientation

panels.purpose: Show sample-level separation on the first two principal components.

design_rules: use dot size for pathway membership scale and color for directionality
annotate significance compactly without obscuring pathway names
sort pathways so the most interpretable effect-size pattern reads quickly

panels.purpose: Summarize pathway effect size, significance, and directionality in one panel.

design_rules: show ROC and precision-recall together when class imbalance matters
keep model identity consistent across panels with more than color alone
use uncertainty ribbons and explicit operating-point markers rather than headline metrics alone
preserve the prevalence baseline in the precision-recall panel

panels.purpose: Show discrimination quality and ranking with ROC curves and uncertainty at matched false-positive rates.
Show class-imbalance robustness and ranking retention with precision-recall curves and a prevalence baseline.

design_rules: show the identity line explicitly so overconfidence and underconfidence are visually grounded
pair calibration quality with support-by-bin so probabilistic quality is not interpreted without coverage context
keep model identity stable across panels with direct labels, repeated color semantics, and non-color encodings
preserve panel-specific source-data exports for both reliability and support panels

panels.purpose: Show how closely observed event rate follows predicted probability for each model across matched calibration bins.
Show where each model places prediction mass so high-confidence calibration is interpreted together with support.

design_rules: show optimization and validation behavior as trajectories rather than endpoint snapshots only
encode train and validation split with line style so color remains reserved for model identity
label final model trajectories directly and mark best checkpoints explicitly
keep source-data exports panel-specific for loss and validation-metric trajectories

panels.purpose: Show convergence behavior and late-epoch generalization gap across train and validation loss trajectories.
Show how validation discrimination evolves over training and where each model achieves its best checkpoint.

design_rules: row-normalize the confusion matrix so class-wise error asymmetry is interpretable
annotate every cell with percentages and counts so readers are not forced to infer from color alone
pair the matrix with an off-diagonal summary so the dominant failure pathways are explicit
keep source-data exports panel-specific for the matrix and confusion-summary views

panels.purpose: Show class-wise retention and leakage with a row-normalized confusion matrix and explicit diagonal strength.
Show the dominant off-diagonal confusions so the most important failure modes can be read without scanning the full matrix.

design_rules: rank individual features by mean absolute importance so dominant signals are visible without decoding raw coefficients
pair the ranking panel with a zero-centered signed-effect panel so directionality is interpreted separately from magnitude
keep nuisance covariates visibly lower-priority than biologically meaningful features
preserve source-data exports separately for the ranking and signed-effect panels

panels.purpose: Show which features dominate model importance and whether those dominant signals come from biologically meaningful groups rather than nuisance covariates.
Show the direction of top feature effects relative to the predicted response so domain-aligned behavior is visually explicit.

design_rules: anchor all ablations to the explicit full-model zero reference
separate primary-metric loss from secondary-metric shifts so discrimination and calibration penalties are not conflated
encode ablation family and metric identity with different channels
preserve source-data exports separately for the primary and secondary panels

panels.purpose: Show which architectural and training removals cause the largest primary-performance loss relative to the full model.
Show whether secondary-metric penalties are concentrated in ranking quality or calibration quality for each ablated variant.

design_rules: use sparse density-aware labels rather than annotating every embedded point
encode biological state and source domain with separate channels
pair embedding geometry with a support summary so cross-domain coverage remains visible
preserve source-data exports separately for coordinates and cluster-level support

panels.purpose: Show representation structure in embedding space while keeping centroid labels sparse and readable.
Show whether each labeled state region has cross-domain support rather than being driven by one cohort.

design_rules: show the full coverage-risk curve rather than only reporting a selected threshold
draw the deployment-relevant target-risk reference explicitly
mark operating points directly on the curve so threshold choices are auditable
pair risk reduction with retained coverage to reveal excessive-abstention failure modes

panels.purpose: Show how observed risk changes as lower-confidence predictions are abstained.
Show how much coverage each model retains at the selected risk threshold.
